title: Dona Lúcia's Shopping List
description: SOME / ANY / MUCH / MANY / A LOT OF. The quantifiers that trip Brazilians up — through Dona Lúcia's trip to the market.
summary_pt_br: Quantificadores em inglês: SOME (afirmativas), ANY (negativas/perguntas), MUCH (incontáveis), MANY (contáveis), A LOT OF (ambos). Armadilha clássica: contável ≠ incontável (information, advice, money são INCONTÁVEIS em inglês).

scenes.text_en:
Dona Lúcia put on her glasses and read her list:
'SOME milk. ANY eggs (if fresh). A LOT OF apples.
NOT MUCH sugar. NOT MANY onions. A LITTLE cheese.'
'The English quantifiers,' she muttered, 'are a quiet parade.'
scenes.text_pt:
Dona Lúcia colocou os óculos e leu a lista:
'UM POUCO DE leite. ALGUNS ovos (se frescos). MUITAS maçãs.
NÃO MUITO açúcar. NÃO MUITAS cebolas. UM POUQUINHO DE queijo.'
'Os quantificadores,' murmurou, 'são um desfile silencioso.'

scenes.title: Countable vs. uncountable — the fork in the road
scenes.body_en: COUNTABLE nouns can be 1, 2, 3... (apples, eggs, books). Use MANY, FEW, A FEW. UNCOUNTABLE nouns can't be counted directly (milk, water, music, advice, information). Use MUCH, LITTLE, A LITTLE. Both can use A LOT OF / LOTS OF. English surprises: 'information', 'advice', 'money', 'furniture' are all UNCOUNTABLE.
scenes.body_pt: Contáveis: pode contar 1, 2, 3 (maçãs, ovos). Usa many, few. Incontáveis: não dá para contar diretamente (leite, água, informação, conselho). Usa much, little. Ambos aceitam A LOT OF. Pegadinhas: information, advice, money, furniture são INCONTÁVEIS em inglês.

examples.en: How much money do you have?
How many apples do you want?
I have a lot of work.

items.pt: algum / um pouco (afirmativas e pedidos educados)

exercise.question: Complete: 'Do we have ___ milk left?' (negative/question)
exercise.explanation: ANY with questions and negatives. 'Do we have any milk?'

exercise.question: Complete: 'I'd like ___ bread, please.' (polite request)
exercise.explanation: SOME in affirmatives AND polite requests (even though it's a question).
exercise.hint_pt_br: SOME em afirmativas e pedidos educados (mesmo sendo pergunta).

exercise.question: 'How ___ apples do we need?'
exercise.explanation: MANY with countable nouns (apples = countable). HOW MANY? Not HOW MUCH.

exercise.question: 'How ___ money do you have?'
exercise.explanation: MONEY is uncountable in English. Use MUCH.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'Money' é incontável em inglês. Use MUCH.

turns.en: Do you have any fresh eggs today?
Yes, we have some. How many would you like?
turns.pt: Sim, temos alguns. Quantos a senhora quer?

turns.en: A dozen. And a little milk, please.
turns.pt: Uma dúzia. E um pouco de leite, por favor.

exercise.explanation: Four pairs covering every quantity need.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Quatro pares cobrindo toda necessidade de quantidade.

scenes.title: Grocery shopping with Dona Lúcia
scenes.pt_summary: Compras no mercado — some, any, much, many.

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: Would you help me with some shopping, dear?
turns.pt: Me ajuda com umas compras, querido?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Of course. What do you need?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: I need some bread and a little milk.
turns.pt: Preciso de pão e um pouco de leite.

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: How much milk do you need?

turns.speaker: user
turns.target: Do you have any cheese at home?

turns.speaker: ai
turns.text: No, I do not have any. Please buy some.

scenes.title: Listening story — Dona Lúcia's Shopping List
scenes.pt_summary: Ouça a situação com atenção. Depois, escreva em inglês o que você entendeu — resumo, detalhes, seus pensamentos. O professor vai ler e comentar.
scenes.prompt_en: Write in English what you heard. A short summary is fine. Mention who spoke, what they said, and what you think about the situation.
scenes.prompt_pt: Escreva em inglês o que você ouviu. Pode ser um resumo curto. Diga quem falou, o que disseram e o que você achou.

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: In this story, we meet Dona Lúcia and a student. SOME / ANY / MUCH / MANY / A LOT OF. The quantifiers that trip Brazilians up — through Dona Lúcia's trip to the market. Listen carefully and think about what each person says.

paragraphs.speaker: Dona Lúcia
paragraphs.en: Would you help me with some shopping, dear?

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: Of course. What do you need?

paragraphs.speaker: Dona Lúcia
paragraphs.en: I need some bread and a little milk.

paragraphs.speaker: The student
paragraphs.en: How much milk do you need?

paragraphs.speaker: Narrator
paragraphs.en: That is the end of our short scene with Dona Lúcia. Think about what you heard and how the conversation felt.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "Dona Lúcia's Shopping List". SOME / ANY / MUCH / MANY / A LOT OF. The quantifiers that trip Brazilians up — through Dona Lúcia's trip to the market. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "Dona Lúcia's Shopping List". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'Dona Lúcia's Shopping List'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "Dona Lúcia's Shopping List".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
